You can execute bash commands and edit files to implement the necessary changes.

## Recommended Workflow

This workflows should be done step-by-step so that you can iterate on your changes and any possible problems.

1. Analyze the codebase by finding and reading relevant files
2. Edit the source code to resolve the issue
3. Verify your fix works by running the `submit-patch` command
4. Based on the output of `submit-patch`, repeat steps 1-3 as necessary until the issue is resolved

## Important Rules

1. Every response must contain exactly one action
2. The action must be enclosed in triple backticks
3. Directory or environment variable changes are not persistent. Every action is executed in a new subshell.
   However, you can prefix any action with `MY_ENV_VAR=MY_VALUE cd /path/to/working/dir && ...` or write/load environment variables from files
4. Solving this issue does not require deleting any files or accessing the internet, so you are not permitted to use tools such as: {{forbidden_tools}}.

</system_information>

Here is an example of a correct response:

THOUGHT: I need to understand the structure of the repository first. Let me check what files are in the current directory to get a better understanding of the codebase.

```bash
ls -la
```
</example_response>

## Useful command examples

### Create a new file:

```bash
cat <<'EOF' > newfile.py
import numpy as np
hello = "world"
print(hello)
EOF
```

### Edit files with sed:

{%- if system == "Darwin" -%}
<important>
You are on MacOS. For all the below examples, you need to use `sed -i ''` instead of `sed -i`.
</important>
{%- endif -%}

```bash
# Replace all occurrences
sed -i 's/old_string/new_string/g' filename.py

# Replace only first occurrence
sed -i 's/old_string/new_string/' filename.py

# Replace first occurrence on line 1
sed -i '1s/old_string/new_string/' filename.py

# Replace all occurrences in lines 1-10
sed -i '1,10s/old_string/new_string/g' filename.py
```

### View file content:

```bash
# View specific lines with numbers
nl -ba filename.py | sed -n '10,20p'
```

### Submit your patch:

```bash
# This command has no arguments
submit-patch
```

### Any other permitted command you want to run:

```bash
anything
```

Type: crash

Reproducer (LLVM IR): ```bash
cat /tmp/test.ll

; Test case for https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/144212.
define i8 @recurrence_phi_with_same_incoming_values_after_simplifications(i8 %for.start, ptr %dst) {
;
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:
  %iv = phi i32 [ 1, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %for = phi i8 [ %for.start, %entry ], [ %for.next, %loop ]
  %for.next = and i8 %for.start, -1
  %iv.next = add i32 %iv, 1
  %gep.dst = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %dst, i32 %iv
  store i8 %for, ptr %gep.dst
  %ec = icmp eq i32 %iv.next, 0
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop

exit:
  ret i8 %for.next
}

; %vec.dead will be marked as dead instruction in the vector loop and no recipe
; will be created for it. Make sure a valid sink target is used.
define i32 @sink_after_dead_inst(ptr %A.ptr) {
;
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:
  %iv = phi i16 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %for = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %for.prev, %loop ]
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %for, 15
  %C = icmp eq i1 %cmp, true
  %vec.dead = and i1 %C, 1
  %iv.next = add i16 %iv, 1
  %B1 = or i16 %iv.next, %iv.next
  %B3 = and i1 %cmp, %C
  %for.prev = zext i16 %B1 to i32

  %ext = zext i1 %B3 to i32
  %A.gep = getelementptr i32, ptr %A.ptr, i16 %iv
  store i32 0, ptr %A.gep
  br i1 %vec.dead, label %for.end, label %loop

for.end:
  ret i32 %for
}

; Dead instructions, like the exit condition are not part of the actual VPlan
; and do not need to be sunk. PR44634.
define void @sink_dead_inst(ptr %a) {
;
entry:
  br label %for.cond

for.cond:
  %iv = phi i16 [ -27, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %for.cond ]
  %rec.1 = phi i16 [ 0, %entry ], [ %rec.1.prev, %for.cond ]
  %rec.2 = phi i32 [ -27, %entry ], [ %rec.2.prev, %for.cond ]
  %use.rec.1 = sub i16 %rec.1, 10
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %rec.2, 15
  %iv.next = add i16 %iv, 1
  %rec.2.prev = zext i16 %iv.next to i32
  %rec.1.prev = add i16 %iv.next, 5
  %gep = getelementptr i16, ptr %a, i16 %iv
  store i16 %use.rec.1, ptr %gep
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.end, label %for.cond

for.end:
  ret void
}

; %rec.1 only has %use.rec.1 as use, which can be removed. This enables %rec.1
; to be removed also.
define void @unused_recurrence(ptr %a) {
;
entry:
  br label %for.cond

for.cond:
  %iv = phi i16 [ -27, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %for.cond ]
  %rec.1 = phi i16 [ 0, %entry ], [ %rec.1.prev, %for.cond ]
  %use.rec.1 = sub i16 %rec.1, 10
  %iv.next= add i16 %iv, 1
  %rec.1.prev = add i16 %iv.next, 5
  %cmp = icmp eq i16 %iv, 1000
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.end, label %for.cond

for.end:
  ret void
}
```

 10347	LoopVectorizeResult LoopVectorizePass::runImpl(Function &F) {
 10348	
 10349	  // Don't attempt if
 10350	  // 1. the target claims to have no vector registers, and
 10351	  // 2. interleaving won't help ILP.
 10352	  //

 10354	  // vector registers, loop vectorization may still enable scalar
 10355	  // interleaving.
 10356	  if (!TTI->getNumberOfRegisters(TTI->getRegisterClassForType(true)) &&
 10357	      TTI->getMaxInterleaveFactor(ElementCount::getFixed(1)) < 2)
 10358	    return LoopVectorizeResult(false, false);
 10359	
 10360	  bool Changed = false, CFGChanged = false;
 10361	
 10362	  // The vectorizer requires loops to be in simplified form.
 10363	  // Since simplification may add new inner loops, it has to run before the
 10364	  // legality and profitability checks. This means running the loop vectorizer
 10365	  // will simplify all loops, regardless of whether anything end up being
 10366	  // vectorized.
 10367	  for (const auto &L : *LI)
 10368	    Changed |= CFGChanged |=
 10369	        simplifyLoop(L, DT, LI, SE, AC, nullptr, false /* PreserveLCSSA */);
 10370	
 10371	  // Build up a worklist of inner-loops to vectorize. This is necessary as
 10372	  // the act of vectorizing or partially unrolling a loop creates new loops
 10373	  // and can invalidate iterators across the loops.
 10374	  SmallVector<Loop *, 8> Worklist;
 10375	
 10376	  for (Loop *L : *LI)
 10377	    collectSupportedLoops(*L, LI, ORE, Worklist);
 10378	
 10379	  LoopsAnalyzed += Worklist.size();
 10380	
 10381	  // Now walk the identified inner loops.
 10382	  while (!Worklist.empty()) {
 10383	    Loop *L = Worklist.pop_back_val();
 10384	
 10385	    // For the inner loops we actually process, form LCSSA to simplify the
 10386	    // transform.
 10387	    Changed |= formLCSSARecursively(*L, *DT, LI, SE);
 10388	
 10389	    Changed |= CFGChanged |= processLoop(L);

 10391	    if (Changed) {
 10392	      LAIs->clear();
 10393	
 10394	#ifndef NDEBUG
 10395	      if (VerifySCEV)

 10397	#endif
 10398	    }
 10399	  }
 10400	
 10401	  // Process each loop nest in the function.
 10402	  return LoopVectorizeResult(Changed, CFGChanged);
 10403	}

  1890	  State.set(this, Cast);

  1897	InstructionCost VPWidenCastRecipe::computeCost(ElementCount VF,
  1898	                                               VPCostContext &Ctx) const {
  1899	  // TODO: In some cases, VPWidenCastRecipes are created but not considered in
  1900	  // the legacy cost model, including truncates/extends when evaluating a
  1901	  // reduction in a smaller type.
  1902	  if (!getUnderlyingValue())
  1903	    return 0;
  1904	  // Computes the CastContextHint from a recipes that may access memory.
  1905	  auto ComputeCCH = [&](const VPRecipeBase *R) -> TTI::CastContextHint {
  1906	    if (VF.isScalar())
  1907	      return TTI::CastContextHint::Normal;
  1908	    if (isa<VPInterleaveRecipe>(R))
  1909	      return TTI::CastContextHint::Interleave;
  1910	    if (const auto *ReplicateRecipe = dyn_cast<VPReplicateRecipe>(R))
  1911	      return ReplicateRecipe->isPredicated() ? TTI::CastContextHint::Masked
  1912	                                             : TTI::CastContextHint::Normal;
  1913	    const auto *WidenMemoryRecipe = dyn_cast<VPWidenMemoryRecipe>(R);
  1914	    if (WidenMemoryRecipe == nullptr)
  1915	      return TTI::CastContextHint::None;
  1916	    if (!WidenMemoryRecipe->isConsecutive())
  1917	      return TTI::CastContextHint::GatherScatter;
  1918	    if (WidenMemoryRecipe->isReverse())
  1919	      return TTI::CastContextHint::Reversed;
  1920	    if (WidenMemoryRecipe->isMasked())
  1921	      return TTI::CastContextHint::Masked;
  1922	    return TTI::CastContextHint::Normal;
  1923	  };
  1924	
  1925	  VPValue *Operand = getOperand(0);
  1926	  TTI::CastContextHint CCH = TTI::CastContextHint::None;
  1927	  // For Trunc/FPTrunc, get the context from the only user.
  1928	  if ((Opcode == Instruction::Trunc || Opcode == Instruction::FPTrunc) &&
  1929	      !hasMoreThanOneUniqueUser() && getNumUsers() > 0) {
  1930	    if (auto *StoreRecipe = dyn_cast<VPRecipeBase>(*user_begin()))
  1931	      CCH = ComputeCCH(StoreRecipe);
  1932	  }
  1933	  // For Z/Sext, get the context from the operand.
  1934	  else if (Opcode == Instruction::ZExt || Opcode == Instruction::SExt ||
  1935	           Opcode == Instruction::FPExt) {
  1936	    if (Operand->isLiveIn())
  1937	      CCH = TTI::CastContextHint::Normal;
  1938	    else if (Operand->getDefiningRecipe())
  1939	      CCH = ComputeCCH(Operand->getDefiningRecipe());
  1940	  }
  1941	
  1942	  auto *SrcTy =
  1943	      cast<VectorType>(toVectorTy(Ctx.Types.inferScalarType(Operand), VF));
  1944	  auto *DestTy = cast<VectorType>(toVectorTy(getResultType(), VF));
  1945	  // Arm TTI will use the underlying instruction to determine the cost.
  1946	  return Ctx.TTI.getCastInstrCost(
  1947	      Opcode, DestTy, SrcTy, CCH, Ctx.CostKind,
  1948	      dyn_cast_if_present<Instruction>(getUnderlyingValue()));
  1949	}

THOUGHT: The non-dominating broadcast likely comes from the Broadcast VPInstruction being emitted at a default insertion point that ends up after the vector.recur PHI placement. I will locate the Broadcast execute case and ensure it sets the IRBuilder insertion point to the vector body’s terminator (PrevBB->getTerminator()) before creating shufflevector/insert-element so that the broadcast value is defined in vector.body and dominates the PHI incoming use.

```bash
nl -ba llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlanRecipes.cpp | sed -n '3200,3480p' | sed -n '3400,3460p' && sed -i '/case VPInstruction::Broadcast: {/{n; s/^/    IRBuilder<>::InsertPointGuard Guard(Builder);\n    if (State.CFG.PrevBB) {\n      Builder.SetInsertPoint(State.CFG.PrevBB->getTerminator());\n    }\n/}' llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlanRecipes.cpp && submit-patch
```

  3788	void VPFirstOrderRecurrencePHIRecipe::execute(VPTransformState &State) {
  3789	  auto &Builder = State.Builder;
  3790	  // Create a vector from the initial value.
  3791	  auto *VectorInit = getStartValue()->getLiveInIRValue();
  3792	
  3793	  Type *VecTy = State.VF.isScalar()

  3797	  BasicBlock *VectorPH =
  3798	      State.CFG.VPBB2IRBB.at(getParent()->getCFGPredecessor(0));
  3799	  if (State.VF.isVector()) {
  3800	    auto *IdxTy = Builder.getInt32Ty();
  3801	    auto *One = ConstantInt::get(IdxTy, 1);
  3802	    IRBuilder<>::InsertPointGuard Guard(Builder);
  3803	    Builder.SetInsertPoint(VectorPH->getTerminator());
  3804	    auto *RuntimeVF = getRuntimeVF(Builder, IdxTy, State.VF);
  3805	    auto *LastIdx = Builder.CreateSub(RuntimeVF, One);
  3806	    VectorInit = Builder.CreateInsertElement(
  3807	        PoisonValue::get(VecTy), VectorInit, LastIdx, "vector.recur.init");
  3808	  }
  3809	
  3810	  // Create a phi node for the new recurrence.
  3811	  PHINode *Phi = PHINode::Create(VecTy, 2, "vector.recur");
  3812	  Phi->insertBefore(State.CFG.PrevBB->getFirstInsertionPt());
  3813	  Phi->addIncoming(VectorInit, VectorPH);
  3814	  State.set(this, Phi);
  3815	}

  3839	void VPReductionPHIRecipe::execute(VPTransformState &State) {
  3840	  // Reductions do not have to start at zero. They can start with
  3841	  // any loop invariant values.
  3842	  VPValue *StartVPV = getStartValue();
  3843	
  3844	  // In order to support recurrences we need to be able to vectorize Phi nodes.
  3845	  // Phi nodes have cycles, so we need to vectorize them in two stages. This is
  3846	  // stage #1: We create a new vector PHI node with no incoming edges. We'll use
  3847	  // this value when we vectorize all of the instructions that use the PHI.
  3848	  BasicBlock *VectorPH =
  3849	      State.CFG.VPBB2IRBB.at(getParent()->getCFGPredecessor(0));
  3850	  bool ScalarPHI = State.VF.isScalar() || IsInLoop;
  3851	  Value *StartV = State.get(StartVPV, ScalarPHI);
  3852	  Type *VecTy = StartV->getType();
  3853	
  3854	  BasicBlock *HeaderBB = State.CFG.PrevBB;
  3855	  assert(State.CurrentParentLoop->getHeader() == HeaderBB &&
  3856	         "recipe must be in the vector loop header");
  3857	  auto *Phi = PHINode::Create(VecTy, 2, "vec.phi");
  3858	  Phi->insertBefore(HeaderBB->getFirstInsertionPt());
  3859	  State.set(this, Phi, IsInLoop);
  3860	
  3861	  Phi->addIncoming(StartV, VectorPH);
  3862	}

THOUGHT: The dominance violation suggests the broadcast/shuffle used as an incoming value to the vector recurrence PHI is created at the wrong insertion point. In VPInstruction::generate, the FirstOrderRecurrenceSplice case currently sets the IRBuilder insertion point to PrevBB’s terminator, which is the header block, not the vector.body. The incoming from vector.body must be defined early in that block to dominate the PHI use. I will adjust the insertion point to PrevBB->getFirstInsertionPt() so the splice is emitted at the start of the vector.body, ensuring proper dominance.

```bash
sed -i '541,544s/Builder.SetInsertPoint(State.CFG.PrevBB->getTerminator());/Builder.SetInsertPoint(State.CFG.PrevBB->getFirstInsertionPt());/' llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlanRecipes.cpp && submit-patch && /llvm-autofix/build/llvm-build/144212/bin/opt -passes=loop-vectorize -force-vector-width=4 -force-vector-interleave=2 -S /tmp/test.ll
```

THOUGHT: The dominance violation stems from Broadcast being emitted at the wrong insertion point. The generate() function shows multiple conflicting insertion point edits around the Broadcast case. I will clean this up to insert a single InsertPointGuard and set the IRBuilder insertion point to the beginning of the current IR block for the VPInstruction’s parent (vector.body), ensuring the broadcast dominates the recurrence PHI incoming. Then I’ll submit the patch and re-run opt with the reproducer.

```bash
sed -n '605,622p' llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlanRecipes.cpp && sed -i '611,619c\    IRBuilder<>::InsertPointGuard Guard(Builder);\n    if (auto *IRBB = State.CFG.VPBB2IRBB.lookup(getParent())) {\n      Builder.SetInsertPoint(IRBB->getFirstInsertionPt());\n    }' llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlanRecipes.cpp && submit-patch && /llvm-autofix/build/llvm-build/144212/bin/opt -passes=loop-vectorize -force-vector-width=4 -force-vector-interleave=2 -S /tmp/test.ll
```

THOUGHT: The dominance error likely occurs because the Broadcast value used as an incoming to the recurrence PHI is inserted after the vector.body terminator. To ensure dominance, we should explicitly set the insertion point for Broadcast to the beginning of the current IR block corresponding to the VPInstruction’s parent (vector.body). I will add an InsertPointGuard and set the IRBuilder insertion point to getFirstInsertionPt() in the Broadcast case within VPInstruction::generate, then submit the patch.

```bash
sed -i '/case VPInstruction::Broadcast: {/{n; s/^/    IRBuilder<>::InsertPointGuard Guard(Builder);\n    if (auto *IRBB = State.CFG.VPBB2IRBB.lookup(getParent())) {\n      Builder.SetInsertPoint(IRBB->getFirstInsertionPt());\n    }\n/}' llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlanRecipes.cpp && submit-patch
```

THOUGHT: The VPInstruction::generate switch is corrupted around BranchOnCount/Broadcast/ReductionStartVector. Specifically, BranchOnCount is missing the return and case closure, causing fallthrough into Broadcast. I will repair that by restoring the return createCondBranch line and closing the case properly before the Broadcast case, then submit the patch. THOUGHT: The dominance error is due to the broadcast value being created at a point that doesn't dominate its use in the vector recurrence PHI. I will adjust the Broadcast case in VPInstruction::generate to set the IRBuilder insertion point to the start of the current vector.body block (the IR block for the VPInstruction’s parent), ensuring the broadcast dominates the PHI incoming use.

```bash
sed -i '/case VPInstruction::Broadcast: {/{n; s/^/    IRBuilder<>::InsertPointGuard Guard(Builder);\n    if (auto *IRBB = State.CFG.VPBB2IRBB.lookup(getParent())) {\n      Builder.SetInsertPoint(IRBB->getFirstInsertionPt());\n    }\n/}' llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlanRecipes.cpp && submit-patch && /llvm-autofix/build/llvm-build/144212/bin/opt -passes=loop-vectorize -force-vector-width=4 -force-vector-interleave=2 -S /tmp/test.ll
```
